technique: Oil on canvas (here reproduced on a playing card, the King of Clubs!)
description: Talk about a power pose! Napoleon perches on a rearing white stallion, both of them looking dramatically windswept on a craggy mountain pass. His scarlet cape billows like a superhero cape, his hand points grandly upward toward glory (and probably toward 'over there, let's go'), while his cool, calm face contrasts with the wild-eyed horse who clearly didn't sign up for this. David carved Napoleon's name into the rocks below alongside Hannibal and Charlemagne — because why not photobomb history's greatest commanders? Fun fact: the real crossing happened on a humble mule, but propaganda demanded a fiery steed. And here he's been demoted (or promoted?) to King of Clubs in a deck of cards.
ambiance: Epic, theatrical and gloriously over-the-top — pure heroic swagger with a dash of cinematic drama, like the 1800s version of a blockbuster movie poster.
